findings.attack: Phase StateDeltas were attached directly to resolve_outcome.rate_modulation, so arbitrary floating-point movement masqueraded as simulation.
findings.invariant: terminal forecast comes from simulated terminal WorldStates; no direct probability nudge
findings.remediation: Removed terminal modulation input. Phase outputs now feed causal_state_transition, then causal_outcome_transition writes terminal WorldState; formal institutions consume causal state before their threshold event.
validation: matched-ablation harness now measures terminal total-variation

findings.attack: Unchecked LLM causal_dependencies declarations activated Phases 4/6/7/9/10 on irrelevant questions.
findings.invariant: irrelevant phases explicitly no-op; false causal activation <= 0.10
findings.remediation: Runtime relevance now uses question-only adjudication; compiler dependency output is corroborating provenance only.

findings.attack: The code identified the current hosted model as DeepSeek V3/deepseek-chat.
findings.invariant: exact model identity and temporal tier are audited
findings.remediation: Backend defaults to explicit deepseek-v4-flash; official release/model repository and hashes are recorded. Tier C is selected because no official knowledge cutoff/server revision exists.

findings.attack: A user credential could only be read from an environment variable, making it easy to leak through child-process environments.
findings.invariant: credentials must not enter artifacts or logs
findings.remediation: DeepSeek backend accepts an in-memory credential; the sealed launcher transfers it from parent memory through an inherited anonymous pipe. It never enters child argv, environment, or repository artifacts.

findings.attack: The inherited sealed-resolution JSON is present in the repository tree and protected only by an environment-variable guard.
findings.remediation: The macOS Sandbox profile denies the new split resolution roots and every inherited PR #100 resolution/mapping file. Negative reads are executable probes.

findings.attack: The forecaster currently has ordinary internet access while also calling the model API.
findings.invariant: no live Google/current Wikipedia/open internet in forecasting environment
findings.remediation: The forecast child can connect only to a loopback CONNECT relay; the parent relay accepts exactly api.deepseek.com:443 and rejects other destinations.

findings.attack: The current forecaster reads the pseudonym mapping and the public vault, so identities can be reconstructed.
findings.invariant: no hidden pseudonym map revealing identities in forecaster
findings.remediation: Blinded questions/capsules are built before forecasting; mappings and original bytes remain in denied external roots. The unblinded pool/vault artifacts are explicitly denied.

findings.attack: The inherited vault contains 100 worlds rather than a frozen surplus pool of at least 300; selection and outcome retrieval occur in one builder process.
findings.invariant: event selection frozen before performance; candidate pool >=300; outcomes cannot influence forecasting
findings.remediation: A deterministic outcome-identity-independent pass produced 1,174 independent eligible worlds, then froze exactly 100 before any forecast. Split outcomes live only in the scorer root.

findings.attack: Inherited 40/20/40 splits are hash-based rather than chronological.
findings.remediation: Selected worlds are sorted by question-open time and frozen as earliest 40 calibration, next 20 validation, latest 40 locked test.

findings.attack: Capsule writes and row appends are not fully atomic and a crash can leave partial JSON or duplicate rows.
findings.invariant: resumable immutable manifests and exact row counts
findings.remediation: Capsules use temporary-file replacement. Forecast attempts use immutable keyed shards and atomically rebuilt aggregates; retries remain separate attempts and hashes are reverified.

findings.attack: Baseline coverage is incomplete and current baselines do not prove identical capsule hashes.
findings.invariant: all baselines receive identical admissible evidence
findings.required_fix: Implement all required baselines behind one blinded-row contract and assert per-row question/capsule hashes before scoring.

findings.attack: Tier C blinding can reduce but cannot prove absence of model-memory recognition.
findings.invariant: remaining model-memory risk actively measured and labeled
findings.mitigation: Six probes on every row, visible exclusions, no immutable-snapshot claim, and source/recognition sensitivity reporting.

findings.attack: The frozen representative pool is drawn from one prediction-market provider, so provider-specific listing and participant-selection effects can be mistaken for broad product validity.
findings.invariant: source representativeness and external validity are not overstated
findings.mitigation: Label the benchmark single-source; report domain and market-availability strata; preserve the 1,174-world surplus-pool census; do not claim cross-provider generalization.
